unit.comment: /*************************************************************************** * _ _ ____ _ * Project ___| | | | _ \| | * / __| | | | |_) | | * | (__| |_| | _ <| |___ * \___|\___/|_| \_\_____| * * Copyright (C) Evgeny Grin (Karlson2k), <k2k@narod.ru>. * * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html. * * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file. * * This software is distributed on an "AS IS" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY * KIND, either express or implied. * * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl * ***************************************************************************/

/* The recommended order of the TLS backends: * * OpenSSL * * GnuTLS * * wolfSSL * * Schannel SSPI * * SecureTransport (Darwin) * * mbedTLS * * BearSSL * * rustls * Skip the backend if it does not support the required algorithm */
/* USE_OPENSSL */
/* ! HAS_SHA512_256_IMPLEMENTATION && USE_GNUTLS */
/* OpenSSL does not provide macros for SHA-512/256 sizes */
/** * Size of the SHA-512/256 resulting digest in bytes. * This is the final digest size, not intermediate hash. */
/** * Initialise structure for SHA-512/256 calculation. * * @param context the calculation context * @return CURLE_OK if succeed, * error code otherwise */

function.name: Curl_sha512_256_init

unit.comment: /** * Process portion of bytes. * * @param context the calculation context * @param data bytes to add to hash * @return CURLE_OK if succeed, * error code otherwise */

function.name: Curl_sha512_256_update

unit.comment: /** * Finalise SHA-512/256 calculation, return digest. * * @param context the calculation context * @param[out] digest set to the hash, must be #SHA512_256_DIGEST_SIZE bytes * @return CURLE_OK if succeed, * error code otherwise */

function.name: Curl_sha512_256_finish

unit.comment: /** * Initialise structure for SHA-512/256 calculation. * * @param context the calculation context * @return always CURLE_OK */

function.name: Curl_sha512_256_init

unit.comment: /** * Process portion of bytes. * * @param context the calculation context * @param data bytes to add to hash * @param length number of bytes in @a data * @return always CURLE_OK */

function.name: Curl_sha512_256_update

unit.comment: /** * Finalise SHA-512/256 calculation, return digest. * * @param context the calculation context * @param[out] digest set to the hash, must be #SHA512_256_DIGEST_SIZE bytes * @return always CURLE_OK */
/* No system or TLS backend SHA-512/256 implementation available */
/* Use local implementation */

/* ** This implementation of SHA-512/256 hash calculation was originally ** * * ** written by Evgeny Grin (Karlson2k) for GNU libmicrohttpd. ** * * ** The author ported the code to libcurl. The ported code is provided ** * * ** under curl license. ** * * ** This is a minimal version with minimal optimisations. Performance ** * * ** can be significantly improved. Big-endian store and load macros ** * * ** are obvious targets for optimisation. ** */
/* empty */
/* Assume that 'inline' macro was already defined correctly by * the build system. */
/* The code is compiled with C++ compiler. * C++ always supports 'inline'. */
/* C99 (and later) supports 'inline' keyword */
/* GCC supports '__inline__' as an extension */
/* empty */
/* Bits manipulation macros and functions. Can be moved to other headers to reuse. */
unit.cpp:define: #define MHDX_GET_64BIT_BE(ptr) \ ( ((curl_uint64_t)(((const unsigned char*)(ptr))[0]) << 56) | \ ((curl_uint64_t)(((const unsigned char*)(ptr))[1]) << 48) | \ ((curl_uint64_t)(((const unsigned char*)(ptr))[2]) << 40) | \ ((curl_uint64_t)(((const unsigned char*)(ptr))[3]) << 32) | \ ((curl_uint64_t)(((const unsigned char*)(ptr))[4]) << 24) | \ ((curl_uint64_t)(((const unsigned char*)(ptr))[5]) << 16) | \ ((curl_uint64_t)(((const unsigned char*)(ptr))[6]) << 8) | \ (curl_uint64_t)(((const unsigned char*)(ptr))[7]) )

#define MHDX_PUT_64BIT_BE(ptr,val) do { \ ((unsigned char*)(ptr))[7]=(unsigned char)((curl_uint64_t)(val)); \ ((unsigned char*)(ptr))[6]=(unsigned char)(((curl_uint64_t)(val)) >> 8); \ ((unsigned char*)(ptr))[5]=(unsigned char)(((curl_uint64_t)(val)) >> 16); \ ((unsigned char*)(ptr))[4]=(unsigned char)(((curl_uint64_t)(val)) >> 24); \ ((unsigned char*)(ptr))[3]=(unsigned char)(((curl_uint64_t)(val)) >> 32); \ ((unsigned char*)(ptr))[2]=(unsigned char)(((curl_uint64_t)(val)) >> 40); \ ((unsigned char*)(ptr))[1]=(unsigned char)(((curl_uint64_t)(val)) >> 48); \ ((unsigned char*)(ptr))[0]=(unsigned char)(((curl_uint64_t)(val)) >> 56); \ } while(0)

function.name: MHDx_rotr64

unit.comment: /* SHA-512/256 specific data */
/** * Hash is kept internally as 8 64-bit words. * This is the intermediate hash size, used during computing the final digest. */
/** * Size of the SHA-512/256 resulting digest in words. * This is the final digest size, not intermediate hash. */
/** * Size of the SHA-512/256 resulting digest in bytes * This is the final digest size, not intermediate hash. */
/** * Initialise structure for SHA-512/256 calculation. * * @param context the calculation context * @return always CURLE_OK */

function.name: MHDx_sha512_256_init
MHDx_sha512_256_transform

unit.comment: /** * Process portion of bytes. * * @param context the calculation context * @param data bytes to add to hash * @param length number of bytes in @a data * @return always CURLE_OK */

function.name: MHDx_sha512_256_update

unit.comment: /** * Size of "length" insertion in bits. * See FIPS PUB 180-4 section 5.1.2. */

/** * Finalise SHA-512/256 calculation, return digest. * * @param context the calculation context * @param[out] digest set to the hash, must be #SHA512_256_DIGEST_SIZE bytes * @return always CURLE_OK */

function.name: MHDx_sha512_256_finish

unit.comment: /* Map to the local implementation */
/* Local SHA-512/256 code */

/** * Compute SHA-512/256 hash for the given data in one function call * @param[out] output the pointer to put the hash * @param[in] input the pointer to the data to process * @param input_size the size of the data pointed by @a input * @return always #CURLE_OK */

function.name: Curl_sha512_256it
function.parameter_list: (unsigned char *output, const unsigned char *input, size_t input_size)

unit.comment: /* Wrapper function, takes 'unsigned int' as length type, returns void */

function.name: Curl_sha512_256_update_i

unit.comment: /* Wrapper function, returns void */

function.name: Curl_sha512_256_finish_v

unit.comment: /* Wrapper function, takes 'unsigned int' as length type, returns void */
/* !CURL_DISABLE_DIGEST_AUTH && !CURL_DISABLE_SHA512_256 */
